expectations.evidence: Markdown lines 45-72 contain section titled 'Form Field Comparison' with two subsections: 'Budget Line Item Create/Edit Form' and 'Budget Modification Create Form'. expectations.text: table-column-comparison: Output includes a table column comparison section
expectations.evidence: Markdown lines 75-113 contain section titled 'Table Column Comparison' with two subsections: 'Main Budget Table' and 'Budget Modifications Table'. Shows Procore Column | Alleato Column | Status | Impact format. All 15 core Procore budget columns are listed with complete status (✅ for all). JSON lines 122-150 contain equivalent 'table_columns' object with 'main_budget_table' and 'modifications_table' arrays.

expectations.text: prioritized-tasks: Output includes missing functionality categorized by impact (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
expectations.evidence: Markdown lines 115-142 contain 'Missing Functionality' section with three subsections: 'HIGH Impact (blocks core workflows)' with 2 items (Export budget, Import budget), 'MEDIUM Impact (reduces functionality)' with 6 items, 'LOW Impact (nice to have)' with 8 items. JSON lines 190-283 contain 'missing_functionality' object with 'high', 'medium', 'low' arrays containing formatted task objects with descriptions and priorities.

expectations.text: output-saved: Gap analysis report saved as a markdown file in the outputs directory
expectations.evidence: File 'gap-analysis-report.md' exists at the correct location '/Users/meganharrison/Documents/github/alleato-pm/.claude/skills/procore-audit-workspace/iteration-1/eval-0-budget-audit/with_skill/outputs/gap-analysis-report.md' with complete markdown-formatted content. Report includes metadata header, executive summary, page-by-page comparison, form field comparison, table column comparison, missing functionality, database schema gaps, and formula verification sections totaling 252 lines.

notes: 2 of 8 assertions failed: finds-budget-crawler and references-analyzer.
finds-budget-crawler: agent used pre-existing crawl output data instead of locating the actual crawler script. The skill may be directing agents toward captured data rather than tooling.
references-analyzer: agent did not reference analyze-procore-features.js. This assertion also fails in the without_skill baseline, making it non-discriminating.
This is the only eval where with_skill underperforms baseline (75% vs 87.5%). The 12.5% gap is entirely attributable to finds-budget-crawler.

notes: All 6 assertions passed (100%). Non-discriminating: baseline also achieves 100%.
OVERALL: Skill underperforms baseline by 4 percentage points (91.67% vs 95.83%).
DISCRIMINATING EVAL: Only eval-0 (budget-audit) differentiates configs. Evals 1 and 2 show 100% for both, providing no signal.

KEY FAILURE: finds-budget-crawler passes 100% without-skill but fails 100% with-skill. The skill appears to redirect agents toward pre-existing crawl data rather than toward the crawler scripts themselves.
NON-DISCRIMINATING ASSERTION: references-analyzer fails 100% in both configs for eval-0. It should be revised or removed.
COST: Skill adds ~9,574 tokens (+6.5%) and ~48.8s (+10.7%) per run with no accuracy benefit.
VARIANCE: Skill reduces time stddev (30.2s vs 172.7s), suggesting more consistent but slower execution.

ITERATION-2 PRIORITIES: (1) Fix skill to direct agents to crawler scripts not crawl output data, (2) add explicit instruction to use analyze-procore-features.js, (3) add harder assertions to evals 1 and 2 to create discriminating signal.
